title: gouldia terminalis var. cordata
intro: Gouldia terminalis var. cordata, also known as the Cordate Gouldia or Halaakoa, is a rare, flowering plant endemic to the island of Kahoolawe in Hawaii. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gouldia terminalis var. cordata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against infections, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the leaves and flowers of this plant are typically used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties.
